title: Fix Ed25519 keypair generation in Connect Bot Wizard

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/components/settings/ConnectBotWizardModal.tsx` so the Connect Bot Wizard generates a real Ed25519 keypair (where the public key matches the private key) using TweetNaCl (`tweetnacl`) if available; if TweetNaCl is not available, use `@noble/ed25519` instead, and if dependency installation is not possible, fall back to Web Crypto API Ed25519 key generation.

quotes: Fix the Ed25519 key generation in the Connect Bot Wizard.
Import tweetnacl: import nacl from 'tweetnacl'. If tweetnacl is not available, use @noble/ed25519 instead.
If Caffeine cannot install npm packages, use the Web Crypto API: const keyPair = await crypto.subtle.generateKey({ name: "Ed25519" }, true, ["sign", "verify"]).

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking “Generate Bot Identity” results in a cryptographically valid Ed25519 keypair where the generated `publicKey` corresponds to the generated private key (no unrelated random buffers).
The implementation prefers `import nacl from 'tweetnacl'` and uses `nacl.sign.keyPair()` when TweetNaCl is available.

When using TweetNaCl, the wizard stores `publicKey` as `Uint8Array(32)` and displays/stores the private key as a hex string derived from the 64-byte `secretKey` (`Uint8Array(64)`) returned by TweetNaCl.

If TweetNaCl is unavailable, the implementation uses `@noble/ed25519` to generate a proper Ed25519 keypair; if dependency installation is not possible, it uses Web Crypto API Ed25519 and exports raw keys into `Uint8Array` as needed.

requirements.text: Replace the existing `handleGenerateKeypair` implementation in `frontend/src/components/settings/ConnectBotWizardModal.tsx` with the Ed25519 key generation flow specified by the user: generate the keypair, display the private key as a hex string (BOT_PRIVATE_KEY), and keep the public key as a `Uint8Array` for sending to the backend; keep all other wizard steps, UI behaviors (blur/copy), and the existing `linkMutation` call flow unchanged.

quotes: Replace handleGenerateKeypair: - Generate a real Ed25519 keypair: nacl.sign.keyPair()
Display secretKey as hex string (this is the BOT_PRIVATE_KEY)
Store publicKey as Uint8Array for sending to backend
Keep the rest of the wizard flow unchanged: the blur/copy UI for private key display and the linkMutation call for public key.
Do not modify any other files or components.

acceptanceCriteria: Only `handleGenerateKeypair` logic is changed within `frontend/src/components/settings/ConnectBotWizardModal.tsx`; all other steps, labels, buttons, and mutation calls behave exactly as before.
The generated private key shown to the user is a hex string derived from the key generation output (TweetNaCl `secretKey` preferred).
`handleLinkPublicKey` continues to send the generated `Uint8Array` public key to `linkMutation.mutateAsync(...)` without additional transformations.

requirements.text: If TweetNaCl is not currently present in the project dependencies, add it as a frontend dependency so `import nacl from 'tweetnacl'` compiles and runs; do not introduce any other dependency changes beyond what is required to support the Ed25519 keypair generation fix.

quotes: If tweetnacl is not installed as a dependency: Add it. In the package.json or equivalent, add tweetnacl.

acceptanceCriteria: The frontend builds successfully with `import nacl from 'tweetnacl'` used in `ConnectBotWizardModal.tsx` (or uses the specified fallback when TweetNaCl cannot be added).
No unrelated dependency updates are introduced.

constraints: Use English for any user-facing text.

Do not modify any other files or components beyond what is strictly necessary to implement the Ed25519 keypair generation fix (dependency manifest edits are allowed only if required to add TweetNaCl).
Keep the Connect Bot Wizard flow unchanged (generate -> reveal -> link -> url -> done), including the existing blur/copy UI and the existing `linkMutation` call for public key linking.
Do not edit any frontend paths marked immutable by the system context (e.g., `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts`, `frontend/src/main.tsx`, and `frontend/src/components/ui/*`).
nonGoals: Implementing backend-side Ed25519 signature verification (this is explicitly deferred elsewhere).
Changing the Connect Bot Wizard UI/UX, step order, validation rules, or mutation hook behavior beyond the key generation logic.
